title: ATTRACTIVE VS UNATTRACTIVE BONE STRUCTURE
excerpt: What is beautiful bone structure if you're a clinician who is treating patients or a patient seeking to be more beautiful first thing you need to understand is bone structure the bones or the skeleton are the underlying structure of our face on top of which all the other structur…

segments.text: what is beautiful bone structure if you're a clinician who is treating patients or a patient seeking to be more beautiful first thing you need to understand is bone structure the bones or the skeleton are the underlying structure of our face on top of which all the other structures sit so they really are the foundation of beauty but not many people talk about what it is about bone structure that makes people attractive in a way that you can use in your clinic or as a patient seeking treatment so in this episode we're going to discuss beautiful bone structure what you should be looking for what is treatable and what is not treatable and what happens when you overtreat or go too far we'll also be understanding the difference between male and female and why this is really at the heart of what beauty is if you're anything like me the first time you inject a cheek a chin or a jawline you suddenly realize you need to understand bone structure what is it that you're actually aesthetically trying to create with every injection many trainers will focus on safety depth and position but what about the aesthetic how do you know when you need to inject when is too much and when it's

just right this is at the heart of Medical Aesthetics I learned a lot about this through sculpting something I recommend all clinicians try at least once if you take the time to sculpt a clay model using what you think you understand you'll see how it plays out in reality and you'll see the gap between what you understand and what there is still to learn back in lockdown I thought I'd use some of my additional spare time that I had to learn to sculpt and I really started to struggle against what the actual details are that make up the human face ultimately proportion is very useful but it really isn't what the structure of the face is made of you need to know something higher level than simply the golden ratio what is the ratio between and it's these points planes and the lines that form the three-dimensional shape of your face which really become useful for everyday injectors on this model on the left you can see the planes of the face which ultimately are what your eye sees our eye really sees the combination of light and shade caused by these planes reflecting a pattern that our brains

turn into something that means human our brains are also primed to make meaning out of that and we're looking for youthful healthy individuals to build relationships with that is ultimately what Aesthetics is really about what do humans find attractive it's Health gender youth symmetry all of these things form a feeling that we get and when someone is beautiful and meets these in a way that we find attractive we want to spend more time with them for many clinicians the first concept we introduced to is the golden ratio this was first identified by Leonardo da Vinci and it's a ratio that we can see all over nature if you look for it I think the best evidence that the golden ratio is useful is simply to look at the proportions of the face the strongest and clearest signal I see is that the width of the intercanthal distance that is the distance between your eyes seems to be the same as the width of your eyes which is the same as the distance from your eyes to the end of your cheek in Beautiful People this also correlates to the width of the nose and the width of the chin in feale females that same intercanthal distance when multiplied by

the ratio of 1.618 ends up being equal to the distance of the chin and the mouth these ratios are incredibly prevalent and you can see them across different races and nationalities and for me that makes them incredibly useful you can make clinical decisions based on this ratio the problem is this is very useful if you're drawing a static image but what if you're creating a three-dimensional structure we need to know more about the details of that structure and this is where bone structure becomes more important what is it about the structure and the shape of a bone that makes it masculine or feminine beautiful or less beautiful and this really is what we are most often tweaking or treating in the clinic environment we are changing the proportions of the defining points of these bones we make them look bigger or more projected or more refined depending on what we do and this is what injectors are constantly trying to hone and do well to do it well we must understand the details not simply the r ratios so let's review the bones of the face these are the bones we're often trying to

emulate with certain injections we're either trying to make them look more prominent or hiding them by blending them in under what will be perceived to be a layer of additional fat so if you wanted to make a forehead for example more masculine you might be projecting the bone more if you wanted to make it more feminine you're hiding these Contours but which Contours are we actually talking about in the forehead we have the frontal projection in a man this is typically bigger and you can see this often quite clearly on me with down lights but it's also important to see that in men we also have a stronger periorbital Ridge and laella line so this dominance here there are two points on either side often where the corrugator meets that are often more angled and then we have the periorbital projection here the bigger these are often the more masculine the face looks the smaller and the more Blended in they are the more feminine the mid face is defined by two main bones we have the Magilla centrally which should be projected relatively strong in the ideal phase and the zygoma which similarly in a man is projected more anteriorly but

in a woman is relatively projected laterally this sexual dimorphism essentially that word simply means the difference between male and female faces which allows us to clearly recognize male and female an essential part of human survival the lower face of course defined by the mandible it has two defining points in a male on the chin and usually one or two very Central points in a female on the chin which are narrower together and then the gonial angle which is wider and broader relative to the zygoma which becomes the defining point of the lower face in men this is a significantly bigger bone compared with a female which is one of the ways the skulls are identified forensically as either male or female these videos have gone viral on Instagram showing the importance of a strong chin and this is one part of the coin if you can create clear definition at the base of the hole of the human head it becomes what I see as the base of the presentation box of the eyes essentially the Apex or the lowest structure of the face that makes everything else look part of that structure in a seamless way the problem

is you can also make chins too strong and this then becomes masculinizing and this is one of the key points to remember if you're thinking about dermal fillers as a solution to Chin weakness is that everything you do to make chins bigger also has the risk of making chins look more masculine which can be your goal but often isn't often we're just trying to get just that balance between definition structure and then overdoing it becoming more masculine so that narrow focal point being very aware of the details the lines and the planes that connect the chin together with the rest of the face can help you make good decisions about what will actually enhance Beauty and not go too far and create a more masculine appearance although strong chins are important for both male and female Beauty it's important to understand the difference between the two a strong chin in a female might project down and anteriorly quite well but it shouldn't be wide and that's really the big difference a male chin tends to be wider the width of the mouth with two angles whereas a female chin tends to be narrower and finished with a rounded or at least two closer

together defining points on either side and this is what makes them more feminine classically textbooks will say that the chin should project no further than the bridge of the nose but if you look at many A-list celebrities they actually break that ratio all the time my observation is a strong chin that projects beyond the nasan is more beautiful in many people certainly more striking or statuesque as opposed to more babyfaced Beauty but what if a patient presents with a strong jawline that's too masculine is there a way of feminizing it well there are a couple of things that you can try or consider in some people the first is have a look at the other defining points of the face what is happening in their cheeks and their nose and is there a way that you can make those structures a little bit more dominant to make the chin softer you can also sometimes blend in the angles to make them less Vivid so if you have a very well- defined angle chin if you rounded it sometimes that makes it less Vivid but I would say anything where you're adding more volume to a structure that's already too big is quite highly likely to make it bigger rather than actually make it softer that

is with one exception can you atrophy the muscles that create some of that definition for some people a strong mentalis might be adding to Chin projection and the master is often part of lateral projection of the jawline and something that you can shrink using Bine toxin to cause muscle atrophy and narrow the face so how do we take all this information and apply it to Medical Aesthetics what's going to happen when your next patient comes into your clinic the first thing is they also need to understand what it is that you're actually aiming for so get clear on what it is that you believe makes up a beautiful chin for example if you're treating a female we want to see certain points very clearly a gonial angle a chin angle and a line or a shadow that runs underneath the jawline that frames the face now if you can make that better with injections then that's what we should be recommending often this is not aable to injectables and a good clinician will give other options as well but if you have a patient who simply needs a more Vivid structure so that you can see them more beautifully

this is what a good injector will provide let's think about what we might do as a technique if you want to make a patient More Beautiful by injecting their chin the first thing is are they male or female male faces require stronger more angled structures and you may be trying to widen the chin and make it squarer with injections that make the defining separate points on the chin more clear and the defining point of the gonial angle and preferably as wide as the zygoma in a female it would be a different approach we still would ideally like to see a gonial angle though it is smaller and of course always narrower than the cheek for many patients you won't be able to see the gonal angle well because of adipose tissue hiding that structure this is not something easily amable to injections although some fat dissolving injections can help a little bit otherwise it is weight loss or other Surgical interventions in terms of treating a female chin we have room to cause a little bit of projection but remember always to make it narrower and not to make the lower third of the chin bigger

than the Golden Ratio or they start to look a little bit masculine even if it's a narrower chin there are three planes to look at anterior projection the width of the chin and the length of the chin any one of those ratios that becomes too big will make the female look more masculine so you really are limited and this should be planned carefully before you start injecting I'm going to show you on this model how important bony proportion is in terms of overall aesthetic we'll see what happens when you overlay aggressively the structure of a male skeleton onto a female face it really is the underlying reason why people have a particular facial shape so have a look in this model as we first start on her jawline the jawline is potentially the most dominant area in terms of what makes for a masculine or a feminine face so as I increase the proportions of the gonial angle of the defining points of the chin of the length and projection of the chin you can see that she becomes increasingly masculine this is no subtle thing it is a dramatic change that would make a huge difference if you actually did this to a patient it would be devastating unless

they actually wanted to look more masculine in a more masculine face the whole width of the face is wider but remember that the gonial angle is still typically equal or greater than the angle of the cheek which is what you can see in this model the width of the nose also makes a face more masculine The Wider it is the stronger it looks the more masculine it is from a side profile this also means greater projection the angle of the nose is also something that represents an area of sexual dimorph we want female noses to be angled upwards as is this model's natural shaped nose it's a very feminine shape as you create a more 90° angle even Beyond it becomes much more masculine the eyes also as you can imagine have many indicators for masculine or feminine if you make the frontal bone and the shapes of the frontal bone more caricature is as in make those natural subtle curves project more you get a more dominant periorbital Ridge the periorbital Ridge in a male is also lower and the frontal projection of the frontal bone is also bigger so as you reveal these structures it is both masculinizing but also associated with some age progression you tend to get

more visible Anatomy as you get older understanding this will also help you understand why sometimes younger women treated with dermal filler in appropriate amounts sometimes look older even enhanced what your brain sees as the underlying structure projecting through which typically happens more with age and this highlight one of the most delicate parts of Medical Aesthetics which is these gentle transitions between structures it's very easy to create an angle on any particular bone a chin or a jawline or a cheek but how do you get that just right so that it flows easily and gently into the next structure without looking overdefined it's over definition that both masculinizes and sometimes ages a face and something that all injectors need to think carefully about in my model this is the connecting planes that we use defining points need to blend gently together with the connecting planes as seamlessly as possible
